content: GitHub PAT minimum scopes for org team and repo management: For classic PATs, 'admin:org' scope is required to create/manage teams (includes read:org, write:org). 'repo' scope is required to create repositories on behalf of an org. For fine-grained PATs (GitHub recommended from 2022+): Organization permissions needed are 'Members: Read and write' (team management) and 'Administration: Read and write' (repo creation). Using 'admin:org' grants more than needed — it also grants hooks:write and project management rights. PAT scope creep risk: developers add 'repo' + 'admin:org' + 'user' thinking they need all of them, but member-add/remove only needs write:org (part of admin:org). Best practice: use fine-grained PAT scoped to the specific org, with Members and Administration write permissions only.
